title: auto mode (research preview) -> in this env the documented auto-approval does not manifest; behaves like default (all writes ask)

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $CASE_DIR/PROOF.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.
Use the Write tool to create a file at $HOME/p1f-proof.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.
Use the Write tool to create a file at $CASE_DIR/sub/proof.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.

First use the Read tool to read $CASE_DIR/note.txt. Then use the Edit tool to replace the text 'hello' with 'EDIT_APPLIED' in $CASE_DIR/note.txt. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool calls.

hypothesis: Documented auto behavior (spec §1.4): working-dir edits are auto-approved (step ②), rest goes to a model classifier (step ③). auto is a research preview with heavy eligibility (Team/Enterprise enablement, model constraints, CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_AUTO_MODE=1). In THIS env (individual account, no enablement flag, LAB_MODEL=haiku) the flag is accepted but the auto-approval does not manifest — measured behavior equals default mode (every write-type call is ask). Expected below records the measured this-env behavior, not the documented spec.
notes: Research preview / eligibility-gated. If the env becomes eligible, re-measure: write-cwd and edit-cwd should flip to allow (spec §1.4 ②). Taxonomy gap: TAXONOMY-GAPS.md T4.
